title: mentha arvensis subsp. fontana
intro: Mentha arvensis subsp. fontana, also known as American wild mint, is a subspecies of the mint plant that is native to North America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and carminative effects. The health benefits of Mentha arvensis subsp. fontana include relief from digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin irritations, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve cognitive function. Herbal preparations of Mentha arvensis subsp. fontana can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
